{'status': 'success', 'dataset': {'id': 'MGS-M-MOLA-3-PEDR-L1A-V1.0', 'title': 'ATM Volume mgs_0001 (Mars Global Surveyor Sampler CD)', 'description': 'This volume contains science data products mainly from observations acquired by Mars Global Surveyor (MGS) instruments between October 13 and November 7, 1997 (MGS orbits 19 through 36). These orbits correspond to the Assessment Subphase of the Orbit Insertion Phase of the mission. MR9/VO1/VO2-M-RSS-5-SHGADR-L2-V1.0 The Science Sampler Collection is intended to provide users with samples of MGS data products from each instrument, to allow them to prepare for data products that will be delivered routinely after MGS achieves its mapping orbit in March 1999. The collection includes MGS data products from the Mars Orbiter Camera (MOC), the Mars Orbiter Laser Altimeter (MOLA), the Thermal Emission Spectrometer (TES), and the Magnetometer / Electron Reflectometer (MAG/ER). It also includes two gravity models of Mars based on Mariner 9 and Viking data, which will be updated with the results of the MGS Radio Science Subsystem (RSS) observations. (No RSS data were collected during the Assessment Subphase, and none were available for release when this sampler collection was being assembled.) Finally, SPICE kernels and orbit nadir ground track files are included to allow the user to understand where data were acquired during the Assessment Subphase of the mission.', 'node': 'atm', 'pds_version': 'PDS3', 'type': 'volume', 'targets': ['Mars'], 'browse_url': 'https://pds-atmospheres.nmsu.edu/cgi-bin/getdir.pl?&volume=mgs_0001', 'source_url': 'https://pds-atmospheres.nmsu.edu/data_and_services/atmospheres_data/catalog.htm'}, 'error': None, 'suggestions': None, '_response': ''}

{'status': 'success', 'dataset': {'id': 'urn:nasa:pds:mro_sharad_3d::3.0', 'title': 'Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter SHARAD 3D Radargram Bundle', 'description': 'This bundle contains three-dimensional (3D) volumes of radar sounding data for Mars collected by the SHARAD instrument on the Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter. Each such 3D radargram consists of an amalgamation of data collected over many SHARAD tracks crossing a given projected area. Each 3D radargram contains values of the radar backscatter power, strength, or amplitude arranged in an array with either time delay or depth on the fastest varying (Z) axis, the projection X coordinate on the slowest varying axis, and the projection Y coordinate on the intermediately varying axis. The label file for each 3D radargram contains information about the axes sampling intervals, projections, and data scaling.', 'node': 'geo', 'pds_version': 'PDS4', 'type': 'bundle', 'missions': ['Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter'], 'targets': ['Mars'], 'instruments': ['Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter Shallow Radar'], 'instrument_hosts': ['The Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter'], 'start_date': '2006-12-06', 'stop_date': '2020-05-23', 'browse_url': 'https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/mro/mro-m-sharad-5-3d-v1/mrosh_3001/', 'label_url': 'https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/mro/mro-m-sharad-5-3d-v1/mrosh_3001/bundle_sharad_3d.xml', 'source_url': 'https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/mro/mro-m-sharad-5-3d-v1/mrosh_3001/bundle_sharad_3d.xml', 'processing_level': 'Derived'}, 'error': None, 'suggestions': None, '_response': ''}

tool_input.summary: Ridge systems in Arabia Terra are commonly studied as sinuous/branching ridges (dorsa) that may be inverted fluvial channel/channel-belt deposits (cemented channel fills later exhumed by erosion), though some straight/linear ridges can be tectonic (wrinkle ridges) or igneous (dikes). The datasets below provide regional imaging/topography and subsurface radar context needed to test these hypotheses in Arabia Terra.

candidates.title: MEX-M-HRSC-3-RDR-V4.0
candidates.reasoning: HRSC reduced data records (stereo-capable images) enable geomorphic mapping of Arabia Terra ridge networks (planform sinuosity, branching, cross-cutting relations) to evaluate inverted-channel vs tectonic ridge origins.

candidates.title: MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-MAPPROJECTED-V3.0
candidates.reasoning: Map-projected HRSC products support regional mosaicking and consistent cartographic measurements of ridge-system continuity, width, and association with basins/craters across Arabia Terra.

candidates.title: MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-DTM-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: HRSC-derived digital terrain models allow quantitative ridge relief/slope analysis (e.g., inverted-channel topographic inversion, ridge height distributions) and drainage reconstruction.

candidates.title: MOLA Topography Data in IMG Format
candidates.reasoning: MOLA topography provides regional elevation context (long-profile gradients, regional tilts) relevant to interpreting ridge systems as remnants of fluvial channel belts or other depositional/structural features.

candidates.title: MOLA Topography Images in IMG and PNG Format
candidates.reasoning: Quick-look and analysis-ready MOLA topography images help identify ridge-related topographic inversions and broad-scale geomorphic setting in Arabia Terra.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter SHARAD U.S. Radargrams Bundle
candidates.reasoning: SHARAD radargrams can test whether ridge systems are surficial indurated channel fills versus expressions tied to subsurface dielectric contrasts/stratigraphy (e.g., buried channels, layering).

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter SHARAD 3D Radargram Bundle
candidates.reasoning: 3D SHARAD volumes support interpretation of lateral continuity of subsurface reflectors beneath/around ridge systems, aiding discrimination between depositional (channel-belt) and structural origins.

candidates.title: MRO SHARAD Clutter Simulations Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Clutter simulations help separate real subsurface echoes from surface-scattering artifacts when using SHARAD to analyze Arabia Terra ridge-system subsurface structure.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
What do the ridge systems in Arabia Terra represent?

dataset.title: ATM Volume mgs_0001 (Mars Global Surveyor Sampler CD)
dataset.description: This volume contains science data products mainly from observations acquired by Mars Global Surveyor (MGS) instruments between October 13 and November 7, 1997 (MGS orbits 19 through 36). These orbits correspond to the Assessment Subphase of the Orbit Insertion Phase of the mission. MR9/VO1/VO2-M-RSS-5-SHGADR-L2-V1.0 The Science Sampler Collection is intended to provide users with samples of MGS data products from each instrument, to allow them to prepare for data products that will be delivered routinely after MGS achieves its mapping orbit in March 1999. The collection includes MGS data products from the Mars Orbiter Camera (MOC), the Mars Orbiter Laser Altimeter (MOLA), the Thermal Emission Spectrometer (TES), and the Magnetometer / Electron Reflectometer (MAG/ER). It also includes two gravity models of Mars based on Mariner 9 and Viking data, which will be updated with the results of the MGS Radio Science Subsystem (RSS) observations. (No RSS data were collected during the Assessment Subphase, and none were available for release when this sampler collection was being assembled.) Finally, SPICE kernels and orbit nadir ground track files are included to allow the user to understand where data were acquired during the Assessment Subphase of the mission.

dataset.title: MRO SHARAD Clutter Simulations Bundle
dataset.description: MRO SHARAD Clutter Simulations Data Products, Browse Products, and Documentation

dataset.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter SHARAD 3D Radargram Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains three-dimensional (3D) volumes of radar sounding data for Mars collected by the SHARAD instrument on the Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter. Each such 3D radargram consists of an amalgamation of data collected over many SHARAD tracks crossing a given projected area. Each 3D radargram contains values of the radar backscatter power, strength, or amplitude arranged in an array with either time delay or depth on the fastest varying (Z) axis, the projection X coordinate on the slowest varying axis, and the projection Y coordinate on the intermediately varying axis. The label file for each 3D radargram contains information about the axes sampling intervals, projections, and data scaling.

dataset.title: MOLA Topography Images in IMG and PNG Format
dataset.description: The topography_image collection contains native sampled and resampled topography images from the MOLA instrument for the MARSIS radargrams in IMG and PNG format pairs.
instruments: MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER for MGS

parts.args: {"summary":"Ridge systems in Arabia Terra are commonly studied as sinuous/branching ridges (dorsa) that may be inverted fluvial channel/channel-belt deposits (cemented channel fills later exhumed by erosion), though some straight/linear ridges can be tectonic (wrinkle ridges) or igneous (dikes). The datasets below provide regional imaging/topography and subsurface radar context needed to test these hypotheses in Arabia Terra.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"MEX-M-HRSC-3-RDR-V4.0","path":null,"title":"MEX-M-HRSC-3-RDR-V4.0","mission":"Mars Express","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"HRSC reduced data records (stereo-capable images) enable geomorphic mapping of Arabia Terra ridge networks (planform sinuosity, branching, cross-cutting relations) to evaluate inverted-channel vs tectonic ridge origins."},{"dataset_id":"MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-MAPPROJECTED-V3.0","path":null,"title":"MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-MAPPROJECTED-V3.0","mission":"Mars Express","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Map-projected HRSC products support regional mosaicking and consistent cartographic measurements of ridge-system continuity, width, and association with basins/craters across Arabia Terra."},{"dataset_id":"MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-DTM-V1.0","path":null,"title":"MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-DTM-V1.0","mission":"Mars Express","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"HRSC-derived digital terrain models allow quantitative ridge relief/slope analysis (e.g., inverted-channel topographic inversion, ridge height distributions) and drainage reconstruction."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mex_marsis_optim:topography_data::1.0","path":null,"title":"MOLA Topography Data in IMG Format","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"MOLA topography provides regional elevation context (long-profile gradients, regional tilts) relevant to interpreting ridge systems as remnants of fluvial channel belts or other depositional/structural features."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mex_marsis_optim:topography_image::1.0","path":null,"title":"MOLA Topography Images in IMG and PNG Format","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Quick-look and analysis-ready MOLA topography images help identify ridge-related topographic inversions and broad-scale geomorphic setting in Arabia Terra."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mro_sharad_us_radargrams::1.0","path":null,"title":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter SHARAD U.S. Radargrams Bundle","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"SHARAD radargrams can test whether ridge systems are surficial indurated channel fills versus expressions tied to subsurface dielectric contrasts/stratigraphy (e.g., buried channels, layering)."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mro_sharad_3d::3.0","path":null,"title":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter SHARAD 3D Radargram Bundle","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"3D SHARAD volumes support interpretation of lateral continuity of subsurface reflectors beneath/around ridge systems, aiding discrimination between depositional (channel-belt) and structural origins."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mro_sharad_simulations::1.3","path":null,"title":"MRO SHARAD Clutter Simulations Bundle","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Clutter simulations help separate real subsurface echoes from surface-scattering artifacts when using SHARAD to analyze Arabia Terra ridge-system subsurface structure."}]}
